channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Trump’s billionaire pick to lead NASA sparks debate over the future of space exploration
item.description: Jared Isaacman, billionaire, CEO and nominee to become the next NASA administrator, faced questions on April 9, 2025, from members of the Senate Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation during his confirmation hearing for the position.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: iPhones made in the USA? Here’s why that won’t happen, even with Trump’s tariffs on China
item.description: President Donald Trump’s administration has been predicting its barrage of tariffs targeting China will push Apple into manufacturing the iPhone in the United States for the first time. But that’s an unlikely scenario even with U.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Theranos fraudster Elizabeth Holmes keeps fighting, claims appeal judges were confused, illogical and factually mistaken
item.description: The jury in Holmes’ trial heard evidence that she doctored internal Theranos documents and that she and Theranos had falsely suggested to investors that her machines were in battlefield use.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: This tiny screw is powering the humanoid robot revolution
item.description: The humanoid robotics revolution is just around the corner. Test models are already working in factories alongside human beings across the world, while AI companies develop new foundation models designed to help robots navigate their environments as easily as humans do.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Doctors remove pig kidney from an Alabama woman after a record 130 days
item.description: An Alabama woman who lived with a pig kidney for a record 130 days had the organ removed and is back on dialysis.
item.dc:creator: Lauran Neergaard, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Singapore man accused of chip fraud wields Silicon Valley and global connections
item.description: The high-profile case could cast light on how US technology subject to export restrictions is funneled around the planet.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg News, The Mercury News

item.title: WhatsApp has just dropped these 9 new features – including 2 that I'm not happy about
item.description: WhatsApp has just rolled out an endless list of new call and chat functions, but two of them have really rubbed users the wrong way.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: Google Cloud Next 2025: News and insights
item.description: Google Cloud Next ’25, which runs from April 9-11 in Las Vegas is highlighting the latest advancements and future directions of Google Cloud Platform and cloud computing.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Silicon Valley needs to get back to silicon
item.description: With their drab gray suits and their Buddy Holly glasses, the so-called traitorous eight don’t look like revolutionaries. Given no context, you can imagine them occupying some kind of middle-management role at a small regional bank. And yet these are the people you can thank for the digital world.
item.dc:creator: Rodrigo Aramburu, Fast Company

item.title: Amazon CEO says it has to operate like the “world's largest startup”, urges AI investment
item.description: Jassy sees seven commonalities between Amazon and startups as he spells out the future for the company.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Google to offer its Workspace suite to the US government at a 71% discount
item.description: In a move that could reshape public-sector IT procurement, Google has entered into a landmark agreement with the US General Services Administration (GSA), offering its Workspace productivity suite to all federal agencies at discounts of up to 71%.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Netflix’s next Jane Austen series is shaping up to be a better adaptation than Persuasion after landing Emma Corrin, Olivia Coleman and Jack Lowden
item.description: "Better than Persuasion" may be a low bar, but we're optimistic about Netflix's latest Jane Austen adaptation.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Defining leadership through mentorship and a strong network
item.description: Andrea Schulze understands the importance of mentorship when it comes to fostering more gender diversity in the tech industry, especially up to the leadership level.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Google's new Android update will give your devices a battery boost – plus a helpful new feature for Google Wallet
item.description: There's a new update to Google Play services heading to devices, and we should see some tangible benefits.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: DOGE just announced more layoffs at TTS, the government’s vaunted tech group, sources say
item.description: Thomas Shedd, who directs the General Services Administration’s vaunted tech group, Technology Transformation Services (TTS), has just announced that a new round of firings has begun at TTS, insiders tells Fast Company.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Amazon CEO: Sellers will try to pass tariff costs along to buyers
item.description: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy predicted that the retailer’s web of third-party sellers will try to pass the extra costs incurred by the Trump administration's wide-ranging tariffs on to buyers.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: How Camb.ai is breaking language barriers with AI
item.description: Camb.ai is on a mission to disrupt the dominance of English in global media. Founded in 2022, the AI-powered platform specializes in real-time translation that retains a speaker’s emotional resonance—processing content up to 20 times faster than traditional dubbing services.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: The Minecraft Movie: A Game-Changer for Hollywood?
item.description: Hollywood's obsession with video game intellectual property has finally paid off, with The Minecraft Movie becoming the highest-grossing film since Deadpool & Wolverine.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Nvidia's Unannounced RTX 5060 Ti Leaks Performance Numbers - A Potential Game-Changer?
item.description: A recent leak reveals the Nvidia RTX 5060 Ti is up to 14% faster than its predecessor, but will it stay at retail price? The GPU market is in a precarious state, and scalpers are taking advantage of the situation.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: GiveSendGo defends fundraiser for Texas track meet stabbing suspect
item.description: Karmelo Anthony, 17, was arrested in the fatal stabbing of another Texas high school student, Austin Metcalf.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: House Democrats warn Musk must leave government role by May 30
item.description: Dozens of House Democrats are demanding President Trump confirm that Elon Musk will leave his role in the administration by May 30. A group of 77 lawmakers, led by Rep.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: One Tech Tip: Locking down your device when crossing borders
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Planning an international trip? Travelers should prepare for the possibility of extra scrutiny of their phones when crossing borders, especially when entering the United States. The Canadian government warned travelers in a recent travel advisory that U.S.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: CISA under review: Trump memo spurs scrutiny and uncertainty
item.description: The Trump administration has issued a directive against Christopher Krebs, the founding director and former head of the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) for weaponizing and abusing his government authority during his tenure.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: OpenAI fears irreparable harm from Musk, files countersuit
item.description: OpenAI has filed a countersuit against Elon Musk, accusing the billionaire of a sustained campaign to damage the company and urging a US federal court to block further actions it described as unlawful and disruptive.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft says it's 'slowing or pausing' some AI data center projects, including $1B plan for Ohio
item.description: Microsoft said it is “slowing or pausing” some of its data center construction, including a $1 billion project in Ohio, the latest sign that the demand for artificial intelligence technology that drove a massive infrastructure expansion might not need quite as many powerful computers as expected.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: IETF hatching a new way to tame aggressive AI website scraping
item.description: For web publishers, stopping AI bots from scraping their best content while consuming valuable bandwidth must feel somewhere between futile and nigh impossible. It’s like throwing a cup of water at a forest fire.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: GitHub Advanced Security now offers security campaigns
item.description: GitHub has made security campaigns available for GitHub Advanced Security and GitHub Code Security users. Security campaigns help control security debt and manage risk by enabling collaboration between developers and security teams, GitHub said.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cisco, Google Cloud offer enterprises new way to connect SD-WANs
item.description: Cisco and Google Cloud have expanded their partnership to integrate Cisco’s SD-WAN with the cloud provider’s fully managed Cloud WAN service.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Fortinet embeds AI capabilities across Security Fabric platform
item.description: Fortinet has expanded support for AI across its core cybersecurity platform to protect enterprise customers from evolving threats and simplify network and security operations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Whistleblower tells senators that Meta undermined US security, interests
item.description: Sarah Wynn-Williams, the former director of global public policy at Facebook, which is now called Meta, testified at a Senate hearing Wednesday that she saw Meta executives “repeatedly undermine U.S. national security and betray American values” during her seven-year stint in that job.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, The Hill

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: The dangers of oversharing with AI tools
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: One Tech Tip: Locking down your device when crossing borders
item.description: Travelers should prepare for extra scrutiny of devices when crossing borders, especially when entering the U.S. Experts say 100% privacy might be impossible but there things you can do to make it harder for someone to see your private data.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Google Cloud introduces cloud app design center
item.description: Google Cloud has introduced Application Design Center, a service that helps platform administrators and developers design, deploy, and manage applications on the Google Cloud Platform. Application Design Center is in public preview.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: What is Kubernetes? Scalable cloud-native applications
item.description: Kubernetes is a popular open source platform for container orchestration—that is, for managing applications built from multiple, largely self-contained runtimes called containers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Benchmarking AI: Red Flags and Due Diligence
item.description: The accuracy and relevancy of benchmarking have been raised due to the revelation that Meta misled users about its Llama 4 model's performance, highlighting the need for due diligence when evaluating AI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Scientists genetically engineer wolves with white hair and muscular jaws like the extinct dire wolf
item.description: Three genetically engineered wolves that may resemble extinct dire wolves are trotting, sleeping and howling in an undisclosed secure location in the U.S., according to the company that aims to bring back lost species.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Android feature prevents sensitive content in Google Messages
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson talks about how Google kept Android SafetyCore details quiet until it scanned users' photos.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China’s rare earth export controls threaten enterprise IT hardware supply chains
item.description: China has announced immediate export controls on seven more rare earth elements critical to enterprise IT hardware manufacturing, firing a fresh salvo in the ongoing tech trade war.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft workers say they've been fired after 50th anniversary protest over Israel contract
item.description: Microsoft has fired two employees who interrupted the company's 50th anniversary celebration to protest its work supplying artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military, according to a group representing the workers.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The best ways to give your old Android a second life
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explains how you can creatively repurpose your old unused Android devices, which are full of potential and hidden value.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
